Patch Set 7:

It doesn't absolutely need a rebase.  The only thing changed it its dependency is a sles12 patch that isn't used, the fc20 series has its own version.  At some point I will rebase it, but it's not an urgent necessity.

Patch Set 8:

I'm seeing the following compile error with 3.15.4-200.fc20.el6

Applying ext4 patches: fc20/ext4-inode-version.patch sles12/ext4-lookup-dotdot.patch rhel6.3/ext4-print-inum-in-htree-warning.patch fc20/ext4-prealloc.patch fc20/ext4-mballoc-extra-checks.patch1 out of 20 hunks FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/mballoc.c.rej

Have we moved to a newer version?

Patch Set 8:

yes, we have moved to a later version.  current version in fc20 is 3.15.8-200. we only maintain ldiskfs patches for the latest version.  context changes between 3.5.14 and now required slight changes.

Patch Set 8:

typo.  I meant 3.15.4 in previous comment, not 3.5.14.
Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 8:

This is really old. We should abandon it.

Abandoned

agreed this is very old and hasn't been maintained.  probably no piece of it could be reused for a newer FCXX version.  Abandoning it.

parents.subject: LU-4416 ldiskfs: enable support for SLE12

commit.subject: LU-5322 ldiskfs: enable support for fc20
commit.message:
LU-5322 ldiskfs: enable support for fc20

This adds support for the 3.15 based kernel in fc20.
It has deviations from the ldiskfs reference patch set for 3.12

Signed-off-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Change-Id: I026d0d2063c1d7f86cf0f2217bb75f830ba52fc4
